skill.name: brandkit
skill.description: Creates premium brand-kit image prompts for logo systems, identity boards, visual guidelines, mockups, and art-directed brand presentations.
skill.summary: Create polished brand identity boards with logos, layouts, palettes, mockups, and visual direction.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 31 high-risk-looking patterns, but review found them to be false positives from Markdown code formatting and design vocabulary. No executable code, prompt injection attempt, network access, secret access, filesystem operation, or data exfiltration instruction was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Markdown Backtick Layout Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The Ruby or shell backtick alerts point to Markdown inline code for grid layouts and logo examples. No executable Ruby, shell script, command interpolation, or user-controlled command path is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible Markdown examples and contain no runtime language context. This makes command execution highly unlikely.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Sensitive and Weak Crypto Text Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The sensitive and weak cryptography alerts match ordinary design language such as description, designer, desired, system, overdesigned, slides, design, and presentation terms. No Windows SAM path, credential store, hash algorithm, encryption routine, or password-handling behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is brand-design guidance, not security tooling or cryptographic code. I found no evidence of sensitive file access or weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Reconnaissance Vocabulary Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reconnaissance alerts match words like system and grid in visual layout guidance. The file does not instruct the model to enumerate hosts, users, operating systems, networks, or local machine details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms are part of brand layout instructions. There is no semantic evidence of system reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Brand concepts often look generic and disconnected. This skill structures image prompts for cohesive logos, layouts, palettes, mockups, and visual guidelines.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured prompts for premium brand-kit overview images.
Defines logo concepts using monograms, metaphors, negative space, and construction geometry.
Guides 3 by 3, 2 by 3, compact, horizontal, and wide board layouts.
Selects visual modes for developer, security, compliance, luxury, voice, and cultural brands.
Specifies panels for logo covers, mockups, color systems, typography, image direction, and system details.
Encourages sparse readable text, disciplined palettes, and consistent brand applications.
limitations: It does not create final trademark clearance or legal brand approval.
It needs clear brand context to avoid generic visual direction.
Generated logos may need human refinement before production use.
It focuses on image prompt direction, not full brand strategy research.

use_cases.title: Launch Identity Concepts
use_cases.description: Create a polished first visual direction for a new product, including logo logic, palette, mockups, and image style.

use_cases.title: Present Client Brand Options
use_cases.description: Turn a short strategy brief into a cohesive identity board that can support early design review.

use_cases.title: Explore Product Visual Systems
use_cases.description: Generate consistent visual language for developer tools, security products, voice apps, or cultural products.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Brand Kit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a simple product description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a premium brand-kit overview image for a new [category] called [brand name]. Use a [tone] personality and include logo, palette, typography, mockups, and image direction.

prompt_templates.title: Strategy-Led Concept
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with audience and positioning details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand-kit board for [brand name]. Audience: [audience]. Product function: [function]. Emotional promise: [promise]. Core metaphor: [metaphor]. Use a [layout] presentation grid.

prompt_templates.title: Visual Mode Direction
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request that selects a category-specific visual mode.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a [visual mode] brand-kit image for [brand name]. Show logo construction, digital application, color system, typography, physical application, image direction, and one concise tagline.

prompt_templates.title: Reference-Based Refinement
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using references without copying them.
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the attached references only for layout rhythm and quality, create a distinct brand-kit board for [brand name]. Preserve the brand metaphor, avoid copied logos, and keep text sparse.

output_examples.input: A developer tool named Framebolt for fast app scaffolding.
output_examples.output: A dark developer identity board with a bolt-and-frame logo, terminal mockup, cyan accent palette, construction grid, app icon, and concise launch tagline.

output_examples.input: A privacy compliance product for legal teams.
output_examples.output: A light editorial board with a seal-based logo, paper texture, deep blue and red accents, document mockups, typography specimen, and trust-focused visual details.

output_examples.input: A voice AI assistant for customer support teams.
output_examples.output: A polished communication system with waveform logo logic, phone interface crop, indigo palette, pulse-ring detail, readable tagline, and restrained product mockups.

best_practices: Provide the brand category, audience, product function, and emotional promise.
Choose one clear metaphor before asking for logo and board details.
Use references for rhythm and quality, not for copying exact marks or layouts.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for many unrelated visual ideas in one board.
Do not rely on long paragraphs or tiny unreadable labels.
Do not request copied real-world logos, trademarks, or brand systems.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates structured prompts for premium brand-kit images with logo concepts, layouts, palettes, mockups, typography, and image direction.

faq.question: Can it design a final production logo?
faq.answer: It can propose logo directions, but final production work should include designer review and trademark checks.

faq.question: Which brand categories work best?
faq.answer: It supports developer tools, security products, compliance brands, luxury services, voice apps, cultural products, and consumer apps.

faq.question: Does it need a detailed brief?
faq.answer: A detailed brief improves results, but a category, audience, and brand name are enough for a first concept.

faq.question: Can it use visual references?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses references for quality, rhythm, and composition while avoiding direct copying.

faq.question: Is this safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no executable code, data access, network behavior, or prompt-injection attempt.
